I see the mismatch source: there are conflicting width rules between shared containers and header-specific overrides. I’m unifying all major wrappers to one responsive container rule and removing header-only width overrides so header/body align exactly.

content.name: ApplyPatch

input.description: Fetch live page after container unification

content.text:
Fixed. The header/body misalignment came from competing width rules. I unified container behavior so header rows and body sections now use the same responsive container system.

### What I changed
In `/var/www/printonet/wp-content/themes/printonet-theme/style.css`:

- Added shared container variables:
  - `--printonet-container-max: 1440px`
  - `--printonet-container-pad: 1rem` (mobile: `0.5rem`)
- Applied one consistent container pattern to:
  - `.col-full` (including header/content/footer)
  - `.printonet-pets-shell`
- Removed header-specific hard width overrides that were making header narrower:
  - from `.printonet-header__top`
  - from `.printonet-header__nav .col-full`
  - from the forced `.printonet-header__top, .printonet-hook-header__top` block

### Responsiveness
- Container padding now scales by breakpoint through the variable change at `max-width: 760px`.
- No component-specific behavior changed outside container alignment/layout logic.

If you want, I can do a final pass to remove the leftover `.printonet-hook-header__top` selector block entirely (cleanup only), but I left it to avoid introducing any further visual risk.
